steps.title: Multiply the functions
steps.content:
Use distribution to multiply each term of $g(x)$ by each term of $h(x)$.
$$g(x)\cdot h(x)=(\frac{3}{8}x+\frac{7}{4})(4x-8)$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Compute the four products and combine the $x$-terms.
$$\frac{3}{8}x(4x)=\frac{3}{2}x^2$$
$$\frac{3}{8}x(-8)=-3x$$
$$\frac{7}{4}(4x)=7x$$
$$\frac{7}{4}(-8)=-14$$
$$\frac{3}{2}x^2-3x+7x-14=\frac{3}{2}x^2+4x-14$$
The answer is D.
